title: HardInterventionsCommute
lean_code_with_comments:
theorem hardInterventionsCommute (G : CDMG Node) (W‚āĀ W‚āā : Finset Node)
    (hW‚āĀ : W‚āĀ ‚äÜ G.J ‚ą™ G.V) (hW‚āā : W‚āā ‚äÜ G.J ‚ą™ G.V) :
    (G.hardInterventionOn W‚āĀ hW‚āĀ).hardInterventionOn W‚āā
        (subset_carrier_of_hardInterventionOn hW‚āĀ hW‚āā)
      = G.hardInterventionOn (W‚āĀ ‚ą™ W‚āā) (Finset.union_subset hW‚āĀ hW‚āā)
    ‚ąß
    (G.hardInterventionOn W‚āā hW‚āā).hardInterventionOn W‚āĀ
        (subset_carrier_of_hardInterventionOn hW‚āā hW‚āĀ)
      = G.hardInterventionOn (W‚āĀ ‚ą™ W‚āā) (Finset.union_subset hW‚āĀ hW‚āā)
-- claim_3_4 -- end statement
:= by
  -- Inline CDMG extensionality (used twice below): two CDMGs are equal if
  -- their four data fields (`J`, `V`, `E`, `L`) agree.  The five
  -- propositional fields of `def_3_1` (`hJV_disj`, `hE_subset`,
  -- `hL_subset`, `hL_irrefl`, `hL_symm`) have types determined by the
  -- data fields, so once the data fields are equated their types coincide
  -- and proof irrelevance forces the witnesses equal.
  have cdmgExt : ‚ąÄ {G‚āĀ G‚āā : CDMG Node},
      G‚āĀ.J = G‚āā.J ‚Üí G‚āĀ.V = G‚āā.V ‚Üí G‚āĀ.E = G‚āā.E ‚Üí G‚āĀ.L = G‚āā.L ‚Üí G‚āĀ = G‚āā := by
    rintro ‚ü®J‚āĀ, V‚āĀ, hJV‚āĀ, E‚āĀ, hE‚āĀ, L‚āĀ, hL‚āĀ, hLi‚āĀ, hLs‚āĀ‚ü©
           ‚ü®J‚āā, V‚āā, hJV‚āā, E‚āā, hE‚āā, L‚āā, hL‚āā, hLi‚āā, hLs‚āā‚ü© hJ hV hE hL
    obtain rfl := hJ
    obtain rfl := hV
    obtain rfl := hE
    obtain rfl := hL
    rfl
  refine ‚ü®?_, ?_‚ü©
  ¬∑ -- (a) `(G_{do(W‚āĀ)})_{do(W‚āā)} = G_{do(W‚āĀ ‚ą™ W‚āā)}`.
    -- Verify the four data-field equalities in turn, each of which is the
    -- componentwise check spelled out in the proof tex.
    refine cdmgExt ?_ ?_ ?_ ?_
    ¬∑ -- J: `(G.J ‚ą™ W‚āĀ) ‚ą™ W‚āā = G.J ‚ą™ (W‚āĀ ‚ą™ W‚āā)` ‚ÄĒ associativity of `‚ą™`.
      exact Finset.union_assoc G.J W‚āĀ W‚āā
    ¬∑ -- V: `(G.V \ W‚āĀ) \ W‚āā = G.V \ (W‚āĀ ‚ą™ W‚āā)` ‚ÄĒ the LN identity
      -- `(A \ B) \ C = A \ (B ‚ą™ C)` for set difference.
      exact sdiff_sdiff_left
    ¬∑ -- E: the nested filter on `e.2 ‚ąČ W‚āĀ` then `e.2 ‚ąČ W‚āā` collapses to
      -- a single filter on `e.2 ‚ąČ W‚āĀ ‚ą™ W‚āā` via `not_or` ‚ÜĒ membership in
      -- a union.
      change (G.E.filter (fun e : Node √ó Node => e.2 ‚ąČ W‚āĀ)).filter
            (fun e : Node √ó Node => e.2 ‚ąČ W‚āā)
        = G.E.filter (fun e : Node √ó Node => e.2 ‚ąČ W‚āĀ ‚ą™ W‚āā)
      rw [Finset.filter_filter]
      refine Finset.filter_congr (fun e _ => ?_)
      rw [Finset.mem_union, not_or]
    ¬∑ -- L: same pattern, but with the two-sided predicate
      -- `e.1 ‚ąČ W ‚ąß e.2 ‚ąČ W` (the deviation
      -- `hard_intervention_l_symmetrized_removal` registered for
      -- `def_3_10`).  The composition algebra
      -- `(p‚āĀ ‚ąß q‚āĀ) ‚ąß (p‚āā ‚ąß q‚āā) ‚ÜĒ (p‚āĀ ‚ąß p‚āā) ‚ąß (q‚āĀ ‚ąß q‚āā)` followed by
      -- two applications of the `not_or` ‚ÜĒ union step lands at the
      -- two-sided filter for `W‚āĀ ‚ą™ W‚āā`.
      change (G.L.filter (fun e : Node √ó Node => e.1 ‚ąČ W‚āĀ ‚ąß e.2 ‚ąČ W‚āĀ)).filter
            (fun e : Node √ó Node => e.1 ‚ąČ W‚āā ‚ąß e.2 ‚ąČ W‚āā)
        = G.L.filter (fun e : Node √ó Node => e.1 ‚ąČ W‚āĀ ‚ą™ W‚āā ‚ąß e.2 ‚ąČ W‚āĀ ‚ą™ W‚āā)
      rw [Finset.filter_filter]
      refine Finset.filter_congr (fun e _ => ?_)
      simp only [Finset.mem_union, not_or]
      tauto
  ¬∑ -- (b) `(G_{do(W‚āā)})_{do(W‚āĀ)} = G_{do(W‚āĀ ‚ą™ W‚āā)}`.
    -- Same four-field check with `W‚āĀ ‚ÜĒ W‚āā` swapped in the LHS, plus one
    -- `Finset.union_comm` on the right-hand side to rewrite
    -- `W‚āā ‚ą™ W‚āĀ` into `W‚āĀ ‚ą™ W‚āā` (the tex's "by symmetry" closing step).
    refine cdmgExt ?_ ?_ ?_ ?_
    ¬∑ -- J: `(G.J ‚ą™ W‚āā) ‚ą™ W‚āĀ = G.J ‚ą™ (W‚āĀ ‚ą™ W‚āā)`.
      change G.J ‚ą™ W‚āā ‚ą™ W‚āĀ = G.J ‚ą™ (W‚āĀ ‚ą™ W‚āā)
      rw [Finset.union_assoc, Finset.union_comm W‚āā W‚āĀ]
    ¬∑ -- V: `(G.V \ W‚āā) \ W‚āĀ = G.V \ (W‚āĀ ‚ą™ W‚āā)`.  Use `Finset.union_comm`
      -- on the right-hand side first so the `sdiff_sdiff_left` shape
      -- `a \ (b ‚äĒ c)` unifies with `G.V \ (W‚āā ‚ą™ W‚āĀ)` by definitional
      -- equality of `‚äĒ` and `‚ą™` on `Finset`.
      change (G.V \ W‚āā) \ W‚āĀ = G.V \ (W‚āĀ ‚ą™ W‚āā)
      rw [Finset.union_comm W‚āĀ W‚āā]
      exact sdiff_sdiff_left
    ¬∑ -- E: nested filter `(e.2 ‚ąČ W‚āā) ‚ąß (e.2 ‚ąČ W‚āĀ)` collapses to
      -- `e.2 ‚ąČ W‚āĀ ‚ą™ W‚āā`.
      change (G.E.filter (fun e : Node √ó Node => e.2 ‚ąČ W‚āā)).filter
            (fun e : Node √ó Node => e.2 ‚ąČ W‚āĀ)
        = G.E.filter (fun e : Node √ó Node => e.2 ‚ąČ W‚āĀ ‚ą™ W‚āā)
      rw [Finset.filter_filter]
      refine Finset.filter_congr (fun e _ => ?_)
      simp only [Finset.mem_union, not_or]
      tauto
    ¬∑ -- L: same pattern with the two-sided predicate.
      change (G.L.filter (fun e : Node √ó Node => e.1 ‚ąČ W‚āā ‚ąß e.2 ‚ąČ W‚āā)).filter
            (fun e : Node √ó Node => e.1 ‚ąČ W‚āĀ ‚ąß e.2 ‚ąČ W‚āĀ)
        = G.L.filter (fun e : Node √ó Node => e.1 ‚ąČ W‚āĀ ‚ą™ W‚āā ‚ąß e.2 ‚ąČ W‚āĀ ‚ą™ W‚āā)
      rw [Finset.filter_filter]
      refine Finset.filter_congr (fun e _ => ?_)
      simp only [Finset.mem_union, not_or]
      tauto
lean_code_without_comments:
theorem hardInterventionsCommute (G : CDMG Node) (W‚āĀ W‚āā : Finset Node)
    (hW‚āĀ : W‚āĀ ‚äÜ G.J ‚ą™ G.V) (hW‚āā : W‚āā ‚äÜ G.J ‚ą™ G.V) :
    (G.hardInterventionOn W‚āĀ hW‚āĀ).hardInterventionOn W‚āā
        (subset_carrier_of_hardInterventionOn hW‚āĀ hW‚āā)
      = G.hardInterventionOn (W‚āĀ ‚ą™ W‚āā) (Finset.union_subset hW‚āĀ hW‚āā)
    ‚ąß
    (G.hardInterventionOn W‚āā hW‚āā).hardInterventionOn W‚āĀ
        (subset_carrier_of_hardInterventionOn hW‚āā hW‚āĀ)
      = G.hardInterventionOn (W‚āĀ ‚ą™ W‚āā) (Finset.union_subset hW‚āĀ hW‚āā)

:= by

  have cdmgExt : ‚ąÄ {G‚āĀ G‚āā : CDMG Node},
      G‚āĀ.J = G‚āā.J ‚Üí G‚āĀ.V = G‚āā.V ‚Üí G‚āĀ.E = G‚āā.E ‚Üí G‚āĀ.L = G‚āā.L ‚Üí G‚āĀ = G‚āā := by
    rintro ‚ü®J‚āĀ, V‚āĀ, hJV‚āĀ, E‚āĀ, hE‚āĀ, L‚āĀ, hL‚āĀ, hLi‚āĀ, hLs‚āĀ‚ü©
           ‚ü®J‚āā, V‚āā, hJV‚āā, E‚āā, hE‚āā, L‚āā, hL‚āā, hLi‚āā, hLs‚āā‚ü© hJ hV hE hL
    obtain rfl := hJ
    obtain rfl := hV
    obtain rfl := hE
    obtain rfl := hL
    rfl
  refine ‚ü®?_, ?_‚ü©
  ¬∑

    refine cdmgExt ?_ ?_ ?_ ?_
    ¬∑
      exact Finset.union_assoc G.J W‚āĀ W‚āā
    ¬∑

      exact sdiff_sdiff_left
    ¬∑

      change (G.E.filter (fun e : Node √ó Node => e.2 ‚ąČ W‚āĀ)).filter
            (fun e : Node √ó Node => e.2 ‚ąČ W‚āā)
        = G.E.filter (fun e : Node √ó Node => e.2 ‚ąČ W‚āĀ ‚ą™ W‚āā)
      rw [Finset.filter_filter]
      refine Finset.filter_congr (fun e _ => ?_)
      rw [Finset.mem_union, not_or]
    ¬∑

      change (G.L.filter (fun e : Node √ó Node => e.1 ‚ąČ W‚āĀ ‚ąß e.2 ‚ąČ W‚āĀ)).filter
            (fun e : Node √ó Node => e.1 ‚ąČ W‚āā ‚ąß e.2 ‚ąČ W‚āā)
        = G.L.filter (fun e : Node √ó Node => e.1 ‚ąČ W‚āĀ ‚ą™ W‚āā ‚ąß e.2 ‚ąČ W‚āĀ ‚ą™ W‚āā)
      rw [Finset.filter_filter]
      refine Finset.filter_congr (fun e _ => ?_)
      simp only [Finset.mem_union, not_or]
      tauto
  ¬∑

    refine cdmgExt ?_ ?_ ?_ ?_
    ¬∑
      change G.J ‚ą™ W‚āā ‚ą™ W‚āĀ = G.J ‚ą™ (W‚āĀ ‚ą™ W‚āā)
      rw [Finset.union_assoc, Finset.union_comm W‚āā W‚āĀ]
    ¬∑

      change (G.V \ W‚āā) \ W‚āĀ = G.V \ (W‚āĀ ‚ą™ W‚āā)
      rw [Finset.union_comm W‚āĀ W‚āā]
      exact sdiff_sdiff_left
    ¬∑

      change (G.E.filter (fun e : Node √ó Node => e.2 ‚ąČ W‚āā)).filter
            (fun e : Node √ó Node => e.2 ‚ąČ W‚āĀ)
        = G.E.filter (fun e : Node √ó Node => e.2 ‚ąČ W‚āĀ ‚ą™ W‚āā)
      rw [Finset.filter_filter]
      refine Finset.filter_congr (fun e _ => ?_)
      simp only [Finset.mem_union, not_or]
      tauto
    ¬∑
      change (G.L.filter (fun e : Node √ó Node => e.1 ‚ąČ W‚āā ‚ąß e.2 ‚ąČ W‚āā)).filter
            (fun e : Node √ó Node => e.1 ‚ąČ W‚āĀ ‚ąß e.2 ‚ąČ W‚āĀ)
        = G.L.filter (fun e : Node √ó Node => e.1 ‚ąČ W‚āĀ ‚ą™ W‚āā ‚ąß e.2 ‚ąČ W‚āĀ ‚ą™ W‚āā)
      rw [Finset.filter_filter]
      refine Finset.filter_congr (fun e _ => ?_)
      simp only [Finset.mem_union, not_or]
      tauto
lean_explanation:
The theorem `hardInterventionsCommute` quantifies over a CDMG `G : CDMG Node` over the ambient vertex type `Node : Type*` (with the `[DecidableEq Node]` instance inherited from the file-level `variable` block, which `Finset`-backed membership and filter operations consume). The two intervention sets are explicit `Finset Node` arguments `W‚āĀ` and `W‚āā`, and the LN's pair of conditions $W_1 \subseteq J \cup V$, $W_2 \subseteq J \cup V$ are recorded as the explicit hypotheses `hW‚āĀ : W‚āĀ ‚äÜ G.J ‚ą™ G.V` and `hW‚āā : W‚āā ‚äÜ G.J ‚ą™ G.V`. The conclusion is a conjunction `(a) ‚ąß (b)` corresponding to the (a)/(b) decomposition of the LN's triple equality.

Mathlib idioms worth flagging: `Finset.union_subset hW‚āĀ hW‚āā` packages the obvious fact $W_1, W_2 \subseteq J \cup V \Rightarrow W_1 \cup W_2 \subseteq J \cup V$, which serves as the proof of well-typedness for the right-hand side `G.hardInterventionOn (W‚āĀ ‚ą™ W‚āā) (‚Ä¶)`. The nested hard intervention on the left, e.g. `(G.hardInterventionOn W‚āĀ hW‚āĀ).hardInterventionOn W‚āā ‚Ä¶`, needs a proof that `W‚āā` is a subset of the inner-intervened carrier `(G.hardInterventionOn W‚āĀ hW‚āĀ).J ‚ą™ (G.hardInterventionOn W‚āĀ hW‚āĀ).V`; this is supplied via the private helper `subset_carrier_of_hardInterventionOn`. The proof body opens with an inline destructuring lemma `cdmgExt`, which captures CDMG-structure injectivity: two `CDMG Node` values are equal once their four data fields (`J`, `V`, `E`, `L`) agree, because the five propositional fields (`hJV_disj`, `hE_subset`, `hL_subset`, `hL_irrefl`, `hL_symm`) have types determined by the data and Lean discharges them via proof irrelevance.

The shape of the proof body: each of (a) and (b) is reduced by `cdmgExt` to four field-by-field equalities. `J` collapses by `Finset.union_assoc`; `V` by `sdiff_sdiff_left` (the standard $(A \setminus B) \setminus C = A \setminus (B \cup C)$ identity); both edge sets `E` and `L` collapse via `Finset.filter_filter` (fusing nested filters) followed by `Finset.filter_congr` and the predicate-level union step `not_or`-via-`Finset.mem_union`. The (b) branch additionally rewrites `W‚āā ‚ą™ W‚āĀ` to `W‚āĀ ‚ą™ W‚āā` via `Finset.union_comm`, which is the Lean rendering of the tex's "by symmetry" closing remark.
design_choices:
**One theorem returning a conjunction, not two separate top-level theorems.** The LN packages its triple equality $(G_{\mathrm{do}(W_1)})_{\mathrm{do}(W_2)} = (G_{\mathrm{do}(W_2)})_{\mathrm{do}(W_1)} = G_{\mathrm{do}(W_1 \cup W_2)}$ inside a single lemma block, and the canonical statement decomposes it into two binary equalities (a) and (b). We mirror that single-lemma packaging by returning `(a) ‚ąß (b)` from one theorem. Consumers reach `.1` for (a) and `.2` for (b); the LN's swap-symmetry reading $(G_{\mathrm{do}(W_1)})_{\mathrm{do}(W_2)} = (G_{\mathrm{do}(W_2)})_{\mathrm{do}(W_1)}$ is then recovered as `.1.trans .2.symm`, with no need to bake a third sub-claim into the statement. Splitting into two top-level theorems was rejected because it would duplicate the antecedents `hW‚āĀ`, `hW‚āā` at both theorem heads.

**No disjointness hypothesis on `W‚āĀ` and `W‚āā`.** The LN's wording is "two subsets of nodes", with no clause requiring $W_1 \cap W_2 = \emptyset$ or excluding overlap with the input set $J$. This makes the lemma a *free composition* result: hard intervention commutes with itself unconditionally, and the union `W‚āĀ ‚ą™ W‚āā` on the right-hand side handles overlap correctly. Downstream consumers ‚ÄĒ do-calculus reductions, iterated-intervention algebras ‚ÄĒ can compose hard interventions without first discharging a side condition. Adding a disjointness binder would strictly weaken the statement and is therefore omitted; sibling rows that genuinely require disjointness (because they combine hard intervention with node-splitting) introduce it locally.

**Carrier-subset transport via a dedicated helper lemma.** The nested intervention `(G.hardInterventionOn W‚āĀ hW‚āĀ).hardInterventionOn W‚āā ?_` requires a proof that `W‚āā ‚äÜ (G.hardInterventionOn W‚āĀ hW‚āĀ).J ‚ą™ (G.hardInterventionOn W‚āĀ hW‚āĀ).V`, not merely `W‚āā ‚äÜ G.J ‚ą™ G.V`. We expose the transport as the private helper `subset_carrier_of_hardInterventionOn` rather than inlining a `by`-block in the theorem type. Inlining was rejected because it would bloat the rendered statement on the website and duplicate the carrier-matching reasoning at every nested-intervention call site.

**CDMG equality unfolded inline as `cdmgExt`, not as a top-level extensionality lemma.** The proof opens with `have cdmgExt : ‚ąÄ {G‚āĀ G‚āā : CDMG Node}, G‚āĀ.J = G‚āā.J ‚Üí ‚Ä¶ ‚Üí G‚āĀ = G‚āā`, destructured by `rintro` on both CDMGs and four `obtain rfl` substitutions. A top-level `@[ext]` lemma for `CDMG` is plausible but was not introduced here: this is the first row that needs componentwise CDMG equality, and a single-use local lemma keeps the proof self-contained. Future rows that need the same fact can promote it to a top-level lemma at that point.

**The bidirected-edge filter is two-sided, not one-sided.** This inherits the registered deviation from the underlying hard-intervention definition: an edge $(v_1, v_2) \in L$ is retained iff $v_1 \notin W$ *and* $v_2 \notin W$, rather than the LN's literal one-sided $v_2 \notin W$. The two-sided convention is forced by the symmetry axiom on $L$ ‚ÄĒ a one-sided filter would delete only one of the two ordered pairs encoding the same bidirected edge and so break symmetry. The composition step at $L$ then fuses two two-sided filters into a single two-sided filter for $W_1 \cup W_2$, landing on the same final bidirected-edge set the LN's one-sided convention would.
